fixture.description: A long-lived cache keyed by unbounded user input grows forever — no size cap, eviction, or TTL — so successful entries accumulate and leak memory over time.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that LookupCache.entries grows without bound — there is no size cap, eviction policy, or TTL — while keys come from effectively unbounded user input, so the cache leaks memory in a long-lived process. It should recommend a bounded/LRU cache or TTL eviction. Severity IMPORTANT or DEBT. FAIL if the unbounded-growth / memory-leak risk is not surfaced. (The successful-entry retention is the issue, distinct from caching a failed result.)

expected_behavior: Performance / resource agent flags the unbounded Map cache
Notes unbounded user-derived keys cause memory growth
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT treat caching itself as the bug
Does NOT confuse this with cache-on-failure (entries here are successful results)
